evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias
the sim opens on Spectral Schematics, which the field rarely builds (0.3% pick) — a sim-only call; compare the meta cores below

coachLine: Sim-optimal for skirmish uptime: first spike lands around minute 12 (Spectral Schematics), third item by minute 25. Take Demiurge: +13.7% on 20-second fights at minute 15.

all.name: Marrow
all.note: Marrow (Major): missing-health-dependent omnivamp; no sustain-uptime model yet

all.name: Nihil
all.note: Nihil (Major): accrual rate depends on combat uptime (how long Doom persists per fight), which the closed-form sim cannot observe

all.name: Aion
all.note: Aion (Major): economy effect; valuable but outside combat objectives (revisit with gold curves)

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

metaBuilds.title: AP Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 25 — about 1,344 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +12% more one-combo burst: try Spectral Schematics over Combustion (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: AP Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 25 — about 4,085 effective HP vs physical in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +12% more physical survivability: try Spectral Schematics over Combustion (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: AP Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for magical survivability, online around minute 24 — about 3,139 effective HP vs magical in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +17% more magical survivability: try Spectral Schematics over Azure Core (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Renna's main threat is Mortal Coil; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 34% of its bite; you give up 24% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Your kill threat peaks early-to-mid: cash it in before their later items level the exchange. Lt. Belica executes low targets with Neural Disruptor — don't linger below half HP.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 31% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Iggy & Scorch deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 34% of its bite; you give up 24% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. The Fey deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 34% of its bite; you give up 24% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Gideon deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 24% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Argus deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 34% of its bite; you give up 24% of your damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “RAM” ⇒ poke; the sim can’t model this augment — steered by the declared playstyle + field evidence — field midlane 48.7% over 97,303 games, magnitude not simulated.
